title: SBS Modified Bitumen Granule Loss in High-UV Desert Climate
failure_mode: Accelerated granule loss on SBS modified bitumen cap sheet membranes in desert climates exceeding 120 kLy/year UV exposure. Granule embedment degrades as SBS modifier softens under sustained high surface temperatures (>170F), releasing ceramic-coated granules and exposing the bitumen matrix to direct UV attack. Progressive cycle leads to alligatoring and membrane embrittlement within 8-12 years versus expected 20-year service life.
frequency: recurring — observed across 15 projects in ASHRAE climate zones 2B/3B, Phoenix/Las Vegas/Tucson metro areas
notes: Higher-reflectance granule colors (white, light gray) show 30-40% slower degradation than dark granules in same exposure. APP-modified bitumen shows better UV resistance than SBS in these climates but has reduced low-temperature flexibility. Specifiers in desert climates should evaluate reflective cap sheet options and consider ASTM D6164 Type I Grade S requirements.
